bayesToolkit 0.1.0

    • Bayes’ Theorem Flu Test Example
    • Introduction to bayesToolkit

bayesToolkit

Bayesian Probability Computation and Visualization Tools for R

bayesToolkit provides a streamlined interface for computing Bayes’ Theorem, generating contingency tables, and producing clear visualizations that support analytical workflows, decision modeling, and probabilistic reasoning. The package is lightweight, dependency‑free, and designed for clarity, reproducibility, and operational use.

Key Capabilities

  • Compute posterior probabilities using Bayes’ Theorem
  • Generate 2×2 contingency tables from prevalence, sensitivity, and false‑positive rates
  • Produce high‑quality visualizations:
    • Grouped bar charts
    • Heatmaps
    • Posterior probability plots
  • Simple, transparent API suitable for analytics, modeling, and reporting

Installation

This package is stored inside a subdirectory of a larger GitHub repository. Install it using devtools::install_github() with the subdir argument.

install.packages("devtools")    # required if not already on system

devtools::install_github(       # installation code
  "mcvanderbilt/code-library",
  subdir = "r/bayesToolkit"
)

library(bayesToolkit)           # load the package

Example Usage 

result <- bayes(
  prevalence     = 0.07,
  sensitivity    = 0.92,
  false_positive = 0.07
)

# Posterior probability
result$posterior_probability

# Contingency table
result$contingency_table

# Visualizations
result$barchart
result$heatmap
result$posterior_plot

Documentation

A full worked example is available in the package vignette:

vignette("bayes-flu-example", package = "bayesToolkit")

The vignette includes: - Parameter definitions - Manual Bayes’ Theorem calculations - Interpretation of predictive values - Visual outputs generated by the package

Testing

The package uses testthat for automated testing. Run the full test suite with:

devtools::test()
